title: Indicators completeness index
description: Per-indicator summary of editorial documentation status, collection completeness, and operator flags. Derived from every artifact under `datasets/indicators/in/**/*.json` by `tools/emit_indicators_completeness_index.py`. Consumed by the `/data-completeness` citizen-facing route. Re-emit after any indicator-content change or schema bump.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema for the `/data-completeness` index. One row per indicator with `id`, `topic`, `title`, `documentation_status` (stub|partial|authored), `inventory_status` (empty|partial|complete), `frozen`, `last_collected_at`, `observed_count`, `pending_count`, `unavailable_count`. Indices change as artifacts change; this is a generated artifact, not hand-edited.

BREAKING: renamed `last_collected_at` -> `last_polled_at` (per TODO/20260517-coverage-temporal-range-plan.md Phase #2; the field is max(sources[].fetched_at) -- when WE last polled, not when data changed upstream). Added optional structured temporal range fields populated from yen_gov.inventory.derive.derive_temporal_range: `min_time`, `max_time`, `min_period_label`, `max_period_label`, `observed_periods_within_range`, `gap_count_within_range`, `time_grain`, `cadence`. These replace the hand-parsed `coverage.temporal` markdown surface in docs/reference/data-inventory.md; consumers MUST read these structured fields, not parse a markdown report (CLAUDE.md §10 anti-pattern). `observed_periods_within_range` / `gap_count_within_range` are omitted for indicators whose `indicator.cadence` is `decennial` or `ad_hoc` (per ADR-0027).

generated_at.description: YYYY-MM-DD date the index was last regenerated. Derived from the maximum mtime of the input indicator artifacts (per CLAUDE.md provenance rule).

path.description: POSIX-relative path under repo root to the source indicator artifact.

last_polled_at.description: max(sources[].fetched_at) from the source artifact: when WE last polled. NOT data-vintage (which is min_time..max_time below). Named `last_polled_at` so operators don't accidentally show it to citizens as a freshness signal -- upstream `Last-Modified` plumbing is a separate arc.

min_time.description: Lexicographic min of distinct rows[].time tokens (publisher's vocabulary, e.g. `2000-04`, `2011`, `2026-05-14`). Per ADR-0027 we do not normalise.

max_time.description: Lexicographic max of distinct rows[].time tokens.

min_period_label.description: Citizen-readable label for the min_time row (e.g. `FY 2000-01`, `Census 2011`).

max_period_label.description: Citizen-readable label for the max_time row.

observed_periods_within_range.description: Count of distinct rows[].time tokens. Omitted when indicator.cadence is `decennial` or `ad_hoc` (per ADR-0027 -- those have no defined cadence so framing observed-vs-expected misleads).

gap_count_within_range.description: expected_periods - observed_periods at the cadence implied by indicator.cadence (preferred) or indicator.time_grain (fallback). Omitted for `decennial`/`ad_hoc` cadence, for `time_grain=date` (snapshots), and when cadence cannot be inferred.

time_grain.description: Mirrored from indicator.time_grain (per-row stamp resolution: year, month, fiscal_year, quarter, date). Surfaced here so the operator can filter the index by grain without joining the artifact.

cadence.description: Mirrored from indicator.cadence (publisher release cadence, optional on the source artifact -- present here only when set there). Per ADR-0027.
